text:
Once upon a time in a forest glade, three magical creatures roamed under a sky painted with dawn's first light. There was Old Owlfrey, the wise old owl with a crimson eye, who knew every leaf and bird song; Orion, a swift nocturnal hawk with feathers like midnight; and Rosetta, a gentle dove who loved reading tales aloud aloud. They hadn *[often]* enjoyed a good read together on evenings like these.

On a particularly cloudy afternoon, Rosetta laid out a thin scroll she'd borrowed from a forgotten library. It was simply a story meant for evenings like theirs. Orion perched atop a tall oak, flicking his feathers back. Old Owlfrey hooted softly. "...Once upon a time, in a land far, far away..." Rosetta began, her voice like velvet against the noise.

Their tale unfolded with vivid descriptions of brave heroes finding hope amidst despair, songs of love that seemed to pierce the clouds, and wise old ow
